reasoning: Both models found PKGBUILD safe and most application code legitimate. Qwen's 'unsafe' flags were false positives in standard components. Token limits caused inconclusive analysis of main.js, but other reviewed files show no malice.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen incorrectly flagged normal Copilot integration binaries as unsafe and misinterpreted base64 usage. Both models failed to fully analyze main.js due to size limits.
coverage_issues: main.js (1.7M tokens) couldn't be analyzed by either model due to context window limits
Potential install hooks or other executables not reviewed
false_positives: language-server.js (Qwen): Normal Copilot binary execution pattern
index.cjs (Qwen): Base64 usage in standard data handling
learnings: Implement chunking/compression for large files like main.js
Improve recognition of legitimate VS Code/Copilot components to reduce FPs
Prioritize review of files actually executed during install over library code
